seed_problem: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.
mutation: composite: generalize the conflict graph to an arbitrary general graph with planted cliques and heavy-tailed vertex weights (input), AND add a global knapsack water-budget constraint on the selected set (output), so the task becomes budget-constrained maximum-weight independent set -- NP-hard, with yield-greedy / yield-per-litre / yield-over-degree / randomized-restart strategies that diverge.
title: Vineyard Irrigation Scheduling: Runoff-Free High-Yield Plots
strategies: single-plot baseline: irrigate only the highest-yield plot (always feasible; equals the checker baseline B)
yield-greedy: sort plots by yield descending, add each one whose addition keeps the set runoff-free and within the water budget
yield-per-litre / yield-over-degree greedy (GWMIN-style): repeatedly pick the plot maximizing w/(d*(1+conflict-degree)), irrigate it, drop it and its conflicting neighbours, respecting the budget

randomized multi-restart with density-ordered greedy fill and best-of-many selection: perturb the density ordering across seeded restarts, greedily fill each schedule, and keep the highest-yield runoff-free within-budget schedule found
